questions.question: Explain how elections help in forming the government.
questions.answer: Elections allow citizens to choose representatives who form the government and make decisions on behalf of the people.

questions.question: Describe two criticisms of political competition.
questions.answer: Political competition can create division and encourage use of unfair practices during elections.

questions.question: Explain the principle of one person one vote one value.
questions.answer: It means every citizen’s vote has equal value in elections.

questions.question: Explain how constituencies ensure representation.
questions.answer: Constituencies divide the country into areas so each area elects one representative.

questions.question: Explain the purpose of reservation of constituencies.
questions.answer: Reservation ensures representation of weaker sections like SCs and STs in legislatures.

questions.question: Why is the voters list important in elections?
questions.answer: It ensures that only eligible citizens vote and prevents electoral fraud.

questions.question: Explain the process of nomination of candidates.
questions.answer: Candidates submit nomination forms and security deposits to officially contest elections.

questions.question: Why do candidates disclose criminal cases and assets?
questions.answer: To provide transparency so voters can make informed decisions.

questions.question: Explain the role of the Election Commission in India.
questions.answer: The Election Commission conducts elections, enforces rules and ensures free and fair elections.

questions.question: Why is the Election Commission powerful in India?
questions.answer: Because it has constitutional authority and independence from the government.

questions.question: Explain two challenges to free and fair elections.
questions.answer: Money power and criminal influence are major challenges.

questions.question: What is electoral malpractice? Give examples.
questions.answer: Electoral malpractice includes bribing voters or threatening them during elections.

questions.question: Explain the role of voters in a democracy.
questions.answer: Voters choose representatives and influence government policies through elections.

questions.question: Why are election results accepted by losing parties?
questions.answer: Because elections are generally free and fair in India.

questions.question: Explain how elections ensure accountability.
questions.answer: Leaders must perform well or risk losing the next election.

questions.question: Why is voter turnout important?
questions.answer: It reflects the level of participation of citizens in democracy.

questions.question: Explain the role of election campaigns.
questions.answer: Election campaigns help voters understand candidates' policies and make informed choices.

questions.question: Why are EVMs used in elections?
questions.answer: EVMs make voting faster and reduce counting errors.

questions.question: What is the role of political parties in elections?
questions.answer: Political parties nominate candidates and present policies to voters.

questions.question: Explain how elections reflect people's will.
questions.answer: Election results show the preferences of voters in choosing leaders.

questions.question: What reforms can improve elections?
questions.answer: Reducing money power and ensuring transparency can improve elections.

questions.question: Explain the role of election observers.
questions.answer: They monitor elections to ensure fairness.

questions.question: Why are elections held at regular intervals?
questions.answer: Regular elections allow people to replace leaders if necessary.

questions.question: Explain the concept of electoral reform.
questions.answer: Electoral reform refers to changes made to improve the fairness and efficiency of elections.

questions.question: Why is transparency important in elections?
questions.answer: Transparency ensures trust and fairness in democratic processes.
